articles.title: European Parliament Member Investigating Spyware Was Hacked With Pegasus
articles.summary: A new report from the Citizen Lab has revealed that former Member of the European Parliament Stelios Kouloglou had his mobile device repeatedly hacked with the notorious Pegasus spyware while serving on a committee that was tasked with investigating the abuse of such commercial surveillance tools in the bloc. "Through forensic analysis of his device, we found that the attackers could have had

articles.title: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills nine, Syrian state media say
articles.summary: There was no immediate claim for the bombing at a cafe close to the Palace of Justice, a major government building.

articles.title: Ukraine: Rescuers work to save trapped residents after wave of Russian attacks
articles.summary: Aid agencies in Ukraine on Thursday rushed to respond to a massive uptick in deadly strikes by Russia, including one of the largest attacks on Kyiv to date.

articles.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that employs a series of clever tricks to infect systems and siphon sensitive data. The stealer, discovered by Jamf Threat Labs, is distributed as a compiled AppleScript (.scpt) file impersonating Maccy, a legitimate open-source clipboard manager. It has been codenamed PamStealer owing to its ability to

articles.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
articles.summary: Threat actors associated with the Anubis ransomware operation have been observed exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777) vulnerability to obtain initial access. "Although tactics differ between affiliates, common patterns emerged in tradecraft through use of legitimate Remote Management and Monitoring (RMM) tooling, credential access, and hands-on-keyboard procedures used for lateral

articles.title: AI Agent Exploits Langflow RCE to Automate Database Ransomware Attack
articles.summary: Security firm Sysdig says it has found what it believes is the first ransomware attack run from start to finish by an AI agent. Its Threat Research Team calls the operator JADEPUFFER and says a large language model handled the whole job: breaking in, stealing credentials, moving deeper into the network, then encrypting and wiping a company's production database. Ransomware has always

articles.title: New ChocoPoC RAT Targets Vulnerability Researchers via Fake PoC Exploit Repos
articles.summary: Attackers are hiding a data-stealing trojan inside fake exploit code aimed at the people who hunt bugs for a living. The malware, called ChocoPoC, travels in Python proof-of-concept (PoC) repositories on GitHub that claim to exploit hot new CVEs. Run one, and it quietly lifts your saved passwords, browser cookies, and files, then hands the attacker a shell on your machine. YesWeHack and

articles.title: As food shocks spread, citizens are showing more leadership than governments
articles.summary: People support efforts to protect forests and curb industrial food production, the results of the latest Global Citizens' Assembly show - but politicians have been slow to act The post As food shocks spread, citizens are showing more leadership than governments appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: With extreme heat now a public health crisis, local data can save lives
articles.summary: The impacts of extreme heat vary widely across countries and cities, and approaches tailored using data can target solutions at vulnerable groups The post With extreme heat now a public health crisis, local data can save lives appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather
articles.summary: Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.

articles.title: Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope
articles.summary: A Nasa-funded robot has blasted off to catch a falling telescope in mid-orbit and blast it back to safety before it burns up.

articles.title: Ukrainian woman named by Interpol as main suspect in Monaco bomb attack
articles.summary: Officials in Monaco say they believe she was "disguised as a man" and may not have acted alone.

articles.title: At least 32 dead after overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in Pakistan
articles.summary: The bus was travelling from Quetta to Peshawar, when it crashed in the Dana Sar mountain range.

articles.title: 'Most massive' Russian attack on Kyiv kills at least 30
articles.summary: The latest barrage deployed the largest number of weapons on the capital and hit locations over a wide area.

articles.title: Ebola treatments trial begins in DR Congo
articles.summary: According to WHO data, there have been 1,406 confirmed cases of the disease in DRC, with 301 suspected cases and 438 deaths.

articles.title: England's warmest June on record following historic heatwave
articles.summary: England saw its warmest June since records began in 1865 and the second warmest for the whole of the UK according to data just released by the Met Office.

articles.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Iranian attack in Bahrain
articles.summary: The 15-member Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Thursday morning to address the escalating tensions in the Gulf region following Iran attacks in recent days on Bahrain and Kuwait while Tehran and Washington continue to trade strikes over the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint. Stay tuned for live updates.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Risk of atrocities in Sudan, UN programme prevents climate displacement, more civilians killed in Ukraine
articles.summary: Drone attacks by advancing rebel fighters who are besieging El Obeid, Sudan, continue to result in civilian casualties, putting around half a million civilians at grave risk of large-scale atrocities.

articles.title: Expanding areas under Israeli control in Gaza increase risks to civilians, UN warns
articles.summary: The continued expansion of areas under Israeli control in Gaza since October’s ceasefire agreement is placing civilians at greater risk and severely constraining humanitarian operations, the UN and humanitarian partners warned on Wednesday.

articles.title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
articles.summary: NASA’s live coverage of the Artemis II mission mission drew unprecedented public interest – including more than 149.4 million views of the launch, lunar flyby, splashdown on NASA-owned platforms, including the 24/7 streams covering the mission and the Orion spacecraft views – demonstrating strong, sustained global engagement throughout the mission. Around the Clock Live Broadcast NASA’s Artemis II Crew Launches to the Moon broadcast set unprecedented viewership records across the agency’s […]

articles.title: ThreatsDay: AI Compute Hijacking, Apple Email Flaw, BlueHammer Ransomware + 14 Stories
articles.summary: This week’s security news is mostly about weak spots. Browsers, bots, sandboxes, AI systems, and email flows all show the same problem in different ways. Everything looks normal until someone tests a small gap and finds a way through. This is not one big break. It is small permissions, weak checks, open systems, and normal tools doing things they were allowed to do. That same pattern runs

articles.title: SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-45659 Added to CISA KEV After Active Exploitation
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a high-severity flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-45659 (CVSS score: 8.8), is a case of remote code execution arising from the deserialization of untrusted data. The issue

articles.title: Unpatched Argo CD Repo-Server Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Kubernetes Clusters
articles.summary: Argo CD, a widely used tool for deploying software to Kubernetes, has an unpatched flaw in its repo-server component that lets an unauthenticated attacker run code, provided they can reach the component's internal network port. Synacktiv, which found the bug, says it can lead to a full cluster takeover. There is no fix and no CVE. The firm says it reported the flaw to Argo CD's maintainers in

articles.title: Tropical forest protection fund at risk after UK stalls on pledge
articles.summary: The Brazil-led TFFF was launched at COP30 as an innovative financial model to keep rainforests standing, but is still short on startup cash The post Tropical forest protection fund at risk after UK stalls on pledge appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Could a heatwave hit Taylor Swift's wedding?
articles.summary: Simon King reports on the dangerous heat threatening North America.

articles.title: Extreme heat and high humidity hitting north-eastern US
articles.summary: Some areas may reach record temperatures over the long weekend.

articles.title: El Niño forecast to intensify, increasing likelihood of extreme weather
articles.summary: More blistering heatwaves and other weather extremes are becoming increasingly likely across the world now and in coming months, linked to strengthening El Niño conditions in the tropical Pacific, the UN World Meteorological Organization (WMO) said on Friday.

articles.title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Stars Sparking to Life in Cosmic Celebration
articles.summary: NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope has captured the infrared light of numerous features that previously were impossible to see beyond the thick dust of the FS Tau star system. In addition to myriad background galaxies that burst into view like fireworks for the United States’ 250th anniversary celebrations, this image flickers with a number of […]

articles.title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
articles.summary: NASA is recruiting research participants for the agency’s next simulated deep space mission. Beginning no earlier than August 2027, research volunteers will spend one year living and working in interplanetary environments at the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, operating under isolated conditions expected during crewed missions to the Moon or Red Planet. Insights from this […]

articles.title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
articles.summary: A first-of-its-kind mission to raise the orbit of NASA’s Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory is poised for launch no earlier than Thursday, July 2, 5:09 a.m. EDT (9:09 p.m. UTC+12), from Kwajalein Atoll, part of the Republic of the Marshall Islands in the South Pacific Ocean. A robotic servicing spacecraft called LINK, built by Katalyst Space, will blast […]

articles.title: Caltech Welcomes Astrophysicist Ray Jayawardhana as New President
articles.summary: Ray Jayawardhana begins his tenure today as the 10th president of the California Institute of Technology. His selection as Caltech’s president, and as the Sonja and William Davidow Presidential Chair and professor of astronomy, was announced Jan. 6. Jayawardhana succeeds Thomas Rosenbaum, who had served as Caltech’s president since 2014. Founded in 1891, Caltech manages […]

articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. articles.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
articles.summary: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. Like Claude Code, Claude Science can autonomously carry out meaningful work when given concise, high-level instructions, and it has access…

articles.title: Google Disrupts NetNut Residential Proxy Network Spanning 2 Million Home Devices
articles.summary: Google has significantly degraded NetNut, one of the biggest networks that turns home devices into rented relays for other people's traffic. Working with the FBI, Lumen, and others, Google's Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) said this week it had reduced the network's pool of usable devices by millions. Google identifies NetNut, also tracked as Popa, as a network spread across home

articles.title: Can giant batteries unlock Africa’s green industrial future?
articles.summary: Battery energy storage systems (BESS) could drive clean tech manufacturing in Africa but shortfalls in finance and data are still limiting deployment at scale The post Can giant batteries unlock Africa’s green industrial future? appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Ocean summit stays silent on new wave of offshore oil and gas expansion
articles.summary: At this month’s Our Ocean Conference in Mombasa, campaigners warned that ocean protection requires governments to halt new offshore fossil fuel projects The post Ocean summit stays silent on new wave of offshore oil and gas expansion appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Cropped 1 July 2026: Heatwave scorches Europe | UK 2050 farm plan | What’s next for the High Seas Treaty

articles.title: On the Strait of Hormuz, BBC finds seized ships and shark fishermen as uneasy calm returns
articles.summary: The BBC travels to the city of Bandar Abbas - the first international journalists to visit the Iranian side of the strait.

articles.title: Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
articles.summary: The ads use terms including “rape” and “child video” and link to content on the messaging app Telegram.

articles.title: Scotland's first gull ranger wants to boost their image
articles.summary: Eyemouth's gull ranger hopes for a more harmonious existence between gulls and humans.

articles.title: Potentially dangerous heatwave to sweep across the eastern United States
articles.summary: A heatwave developing across the eastern United States will bring daytime heat, high humidity and stifling overnight temperatures as Ben Rich explains.

articles.title: Taking the world’s temperature: WHO chief spotlights global health emergencies
articles.summary: The hantavirus global outbreak is officially over, Ebola outbreaks are growing in Africa and responders are helping recovery efforts in quake-hit Venezuela, the World Health Organization (WHO) chief said on Thursday, announcing updates on global health emergencies.

articles.title: Good Morning, Earth!
articles.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams took this photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. In 24 hours, the space station makes 16 orbits of Earth, traveling through 16 sunrises and sunsets. Learn more about the orbiting laboratory. Image credit: NASA/Chris Williams

articles.title: The Download: a smoking “endgame” and a new Elizabeth Bear story
articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m supporting it anyway. —Jessica Hamzelou As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from…

articles.title: The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m supporting it anyway.
articles.summary: As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from mine. The seven-year-old is learning about AI at school. The five-year-old is given internet-based homework every week. And they are both absolutely repulsed by the idea of smoking. That was not the prevailing sentiment when I was young.…
